name: Pebble engine read perf — minimize UnpackReadGrants 1M paginated read

description: Baseline for the read-perf session, starting from the autoresearch/pebble-perf-20260525 branch (WritePack session end state). pebble_readpaginated_1m_ms = 1229.6 ms. Allocs at 1M scale = 17M (versus 22K on the WritePack side — likely a big arena-like win available). SQLite + WritePack sentinels within tolerance.

asi.hypothesis: establish a clean baseline for the read-perf session
asi.allocs_at_1m_anomaly: 17M allocs at 1M scale. The bench paginates 1M grants in 100 pages of 10k each. Each grant likely triggers a few allocations (V3GrantToV2 + nested stubs); 1M × ~17 ≈ 17M matches. Strong hint that an arena-style fix applies (like WritePack session #41).
asi.next_idea: Profile-first: pprof the 1M read to localize the actual bottleneck (NewStore/unpack vs paginated iteration vs Close). Most likely the unpack (ExtractZstdTar) dominates at large scales because the c1z is ~500MB to extract.
asi.iter_time_observation: Full bench sweep runs in ~17s wallclock; much faster than the WritePack session's ~190s. Can afford more iterations per session.

description: grantReadArena for the paginated grants read path with pre-populated nested fields (Entitlement, Principal, DiscoveredAt). Hypothesis: proto.Unmarshal's 'if field == nil, allocate' check would reuse the pre-populated arena slots. Reality: proto.Unmarshal allocates fresh nested messages anyway (allocs only dropped 17.0M\u219216.0M = -5.9%, which matches just the OUTER GrantRecord arena win, not the nested fields). The pre-populated nested arenas are wasted memory \u2014 bytes_op +15.4%, smaller scales regressed +28-40% at 1k/100. Primary flat (+0.5%).

asi.hypothesis: proto.Unmarshal's generated code uses 'if nested == nil { new(T) }' pattern, so pre-populating with arena slots should let the runtime reuse them.
asi.result: Wrong about the proto runtime. Allocs dropped only by the 1M outer GrantRecord count; nested fields still allocated fresh. Smaller scales regressed because arena was sized to DefaultPageSize=10000 regardless of actual records.
asi.rollback_reason: primary flat, smaller scales clearly regressed, bytes_op up.
asi.mechanism_failure: The protobuf runtime's behavior on non-nil nested fields is more complex than the simple if-nil-allocate I assumed. Either (1) it CLEARS the existing nested field before unmarshaling, (2) it MERGES into the existing field but my pre-populated field gets discarded for some other reason, or (3) the generated unmarshal code path for v3 GrantRecord uses a different pattern. Need to read the actual unmarshal code path more carefully.
asi.next_action_hint: Two angles: (a) just arena the OUTER GrantRecord (lose only -5.9% allocs, modest win); (b) figure out HOW to actually share nested allocations across the page — maybe via proto.UnmarshalState directly bypassing the generated Unmarshal, or by writing custom decode for GrantRecord. Also: the arena should be sized to limit OR len(actual_records), not always DefaultPageSize, to avoid over-allocation at small scales.
